title: Refactor CI workflow into reusable scripts and actions
summary: Review #2 feedback from berendt (6 WARNINGs, 4 INFOs) properly addressed. Scope-creep reverts confirmed correct (setup-policies.sh and verify_spdx_headers.sh match main). New hack/ci-resolve-changes.sh cleanly extracts the 30-line changes-resolution block with proper SPDX header, set -euo pipefail, input validation, and CC-0050 reference. sed metacharacter escaping (W-004) and FQDN variant (W-006) correctly implemented. Structural test script (tests/ci/verify_ci_scripts.sh) follows project PASS/FAIL counter pattern with assertions.sh integration. ci.yaml reduced from 680 to 635 lines (28% total reduction from 884); gap to 500-line target acknowledged as follow-up. mktemp fix (I-003) and grep -A30 (I-001) and docs version fix (I-002) all applied. No new bugs introduced.

tests_checklist.item: Test script follows project PASS/FAIL counter pattern
assertions.sh PASS/FAIL globals compatible with test script

code_quality_checklist.item: SPDX headers on all new files
CC-0050 feature reference in all new files
No inline run: blocks exceed ~5 lines in new changes

security_checklist.item: sed metacharacters escaped in ADMIN_PASSWORD substitution
Shell indirection (${!filter_var}) used safely with controlled input

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
New script functionally equivalent to inline code
shellcheck CI job glob (hack/*.sh) covers new script
changes job path filters (hack/**) cover new script

defensive_checklist.item: FILTER_* env vars default to false when unset

issues_found.description: ci.yaml at 635 lines, still above the 500-line acceptance criteria target. This is acknowledged in the review resolution as follow-up work. The remaining gap is primarily from inline blocks in non-E2E jobs (lint, test, docs, helm-validate, build-and-push, merge-operator-images, helm-push, github-release) which are each small (<5 lines) and not worth extracting individually.
issues_found.fix: Document in the GitHub issue that the 500-line target requires further extraction in a follow-up PR, or adjust the acceptance criterion to reflect the practical minimum.

issues_found.description: test_shellcheck() SKIP path (line 50-52) returns without incrementing PASS or FAIL. When shellcheck is not installed, the summary line understates the total test count. Not a correctness bug (the SKIP message is printed), but inconsistent with the require-explicit-failure-branch pattern. In CI (ubuntu-latest), shellcheck is pre-installed so this path is not exercised.
issues_found.fix: No action needed — shellcheck is always available in CI. The SKIP path is a reasonable local-dev fallback.
issues_found.evidence:
if ! command -v shellcheck >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "  SKIP: shellcheck not installed"
    return
  fi

suggested_improvements: Consider filing a follow-up issue to track the remaining ci.yaml line reduction from 635 to <500 if the acceptance criteria is still desired.
The validate-stated-acceptance-criteria-are-met.md review pattern is a good addition — it captures a real blind spot.
next_steps: Merge: all blocking and major checks pass, all berendt feedback addressed.

detected_patterns.name: CI script extraction with env-var interface
detected_patterns.applies_when: Extracting inline shell blocks from GitHub Actions workflow steps into standalone hack/ci-*.sh scripts
detected_patterns.description: Inline workflow scripts are extracted into hack/ci-*.sh files. The workflow step passes GitHub Actions context (step outputs, built-in vars) as environment variables via the step's env: block. The script reads these via ${VAR:-default} with safe defaults. Required env vars are validated at script entry with ::error:: annotation and exit 1. The script writes outputs to GITHUB_OUTPUT. This keeps the workflow declarative (env mapping) and the script testable (env vars can be set locally).

examples.code:
if [[ -z "${ALL_OPERATORS:-}" ]]; then
  echo "::error::ALL_OPERATORS must be set (space-separated list of operator names)"
  exit 1
fi
